system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     rhoCentralFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.2;

deltaT          0.0001;

writeControl    adjustableRunTime;

writeInterval   0.02;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable yes;

adjustTimeStep  no;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

fluxScheme      Kurganov;

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(tauMC)      Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
    reconstruct(rho) vanLeer;
    reconstruct(U)  vanLeerV;
    reconstruct(T)  vanLeer;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    "(rho|rhoU|rhoE)"
    {
        solver          diagonal;
    }

    U
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        nSweeps         2;
        tolerance       1e-09;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    e
    {
        $U;
        tolerance       1e-09;
        relTol          0.01;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";
    object      momentumTransport;
}

simulationType  laminar;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='physicalProperties', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";
    object      physicalProperties;
}

thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         pureMixture;
    transport       const;
    thermo          hConst;
    equationOfState perfectGas;
    specie          specie;
    energy          sensibleInternalEnergy;
}

mixture
{
    specie
    {
        molWeight   11640.3;
    }
    thermodynamics
    {
        Cp          2.5;
        Hf          0;
    }
    transport
    {
        mu          0;
        Pr          1;
    }
}'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='U', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;

    object      U;
}

dimensions      [0 1 -1 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform (5 0 0);

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform (5 0 0);
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            inletOutlet;
        inletValue      uniform (5 0 0);
        value           uniform (5 0 0);
    }

    bottom
    {
        type            symmetryPlane;
    }

    top

        type            inletOutlet;
        inletValue      uniform (5 0 0);

    }

    wedge
    {
        type            slip;
    }

    {
        type            empty;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='T', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

internalField   uniform 1;

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 1;
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            zeroGradient;

    bottom
    {
        type            zeroGradient;

{

}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [1 -1 -2 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform 1;

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 1;
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            waveTransmissive;
        field           p;
        psi             thermo:psi;
        gamma           1.4;
        fieldInf        1;
        lInf            1;
        value           uniform 1;
    }

    bottom
    {
        type            symmetryPlane;
    }

    top
    {
        type            waveTransmissive;
        field           p;
        psi             thermo:psi;
        gamma           1.4;
        fieldInf        1;
        lInf            1;
        value           uniform 1;
    }

    wedge
    {
        type            slip;
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='blockMeshDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

vertices

    ( 0.3048     0.1524   0.005)  // 15
);

blocks
(
    hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) (30 10 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
    hex (8 9 10 11 12 13 14 15) (60 10 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 4 7 3)
        );
    }

    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (9 10 14 13)
        );
    }

    top
    {

        faces
        (
            (12 13 14 15)
        );
    }

    bottom
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 2 3)
        );
    }

    wedge
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (3 7 6 2)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

// ************************************************************************* //')]</foamfiles>

<reviewer_analysis>## Error Analysis

### 1. `log.rhoCentralFoam` — Cast error

**Error:**  
`Attempt to cast type patch to type symmetryPlane`

This is a **new error** that hasn't appeared in previous attempts. The solver is trying to cast a `patch` type to `symmetryPlane`, which means somewhere in the code (likely in the thermophysical properties lookup or mesh reading), it expects a `symmetryPlane` patch type but finds a `patch` type instead.

Looking at the current `blockMeshDict`, the `bottom` patch is defined as `type symmetryPlane`. However, the error message shows it's trying to cast a generic `patch` to `symmetryPlane`. This suggests that **some part of the code (possibly in `rhoCentralFoam` or the thermophysical property initialization) is hardcoding the expectation of a `symmetryPlane` patch name**, rather than dynamically determining the patch type.

In OpenFOAM 10 (Foundation version), the `symmetryPlane` boundary condition is implemented as a specialized derived class from `fvPatch`. When the mesh reader encounters a patch named `bottom` with `type symmetryPlane`, it should register it as a `symmetryPlane` patch type. The fact that it's seeing a generic `patch` type instead means the patch registration or type resolution is failing.

## Root Cause

The issue is that **the `bottom` patch is defined as `type symmetryPlane`**, but the `rhoCentralFoam` solver or the thermophysical property constructor is expecting a patch of type `symmetryPlane` to perform certain operations (like symmetry-based field extrapolation or special boundary treatment). The error occurs during the thermo lookup phase when constructing the `hePsiThermo` object.

## Solution

### For `system/blockMeshDict`:

Change the `bottom` patch type from `symmetryPlane` to `wall` (or `empty` if appropriate for this 3D wedge geometry):

```
bottom
{
    type            wall;
}
```

**Why `wall` instead of `symmetryPlane`?**  
In a 3D wedge simulation with `rhoCentralFoam`, the `symmetryPlane` boundary condition is typically used for 2D simulations where the third dimension is symmetric. In this 3D case with explicit z-extent (-0.005 to 0.005), the bottom face at z=-0.005 is a physical wall, not a symmetry plane. Using `wall` ensures the boundary condition type matches what the solver expects.

Alternatively, if the simulation is truly intended to be symmetric about z=0, then `symmetryPlane` could be kept — but given the user requirement explicitly defines a 3D volume from z=-0.005 to 0.005, `wall` is the physically correct choice.

### Additional consideration

Check the `0/p` boundary condition for `bottom`. Currently it uses `waveTransmissive`, which is appropriate for an outlet/inlet-type boundary. If `bottom` is changed to `wall`, the pressure boundary condition should also be updated to `fixedValue` or `zeroGradient` as appropriate for a solid wall:

For `bottom` in `0/p`:
```
bottom
{
    type            zeroGradient;
}
```

This is consistent with how walls behave in compressible solvers — pressure gradients normal to the wall are zero.

## Summary

| File | Issue | Fix |
|------|-------|-----|
| `system/blockMeshDict` | `bottom` patch is `type symmetryPlane` but `rhoCentralFoam` expects a compatible patch type for thermo initialization | Change `bottom` from `type symmetryPlane` to `type wall` |
| `0/p` | Pressure BC for `bottom` currently uses `waveTransmissive` which is inappropriate for a wall | Change `bottom` in `0/p` from `waveTransmissive` to `zeroGradient` |</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/blockMeshDict', 'changes': 'Change bottom patch type from symmetryPlane to wall'}, {'file': '0/p', 'changes': 'Change bottom patch type from waveTransmissive to zeroGradient'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>Perform a 2D supersonic flow simulation over a wedge. Create a 3D geometry spanning from -0.15242 m to 0.3048 m in the x-direction, 0 m to 0.1524 m in the y-direction, and -0.005 m to 0.005 m in the z-direction. The simulation starts at 0.0 seconds and runs until 0.2 seconds with a time step (deltaT) of 0.0001 seconds. The results are written every 0.02 seconds. The inlet velocity is 5 m/s, inlet temperature is 1 K and inlet pressure is 1 Pa. The wedge boundary uses slip boundary condition for velocity. Use normalized thermodynamic properties where molWeight is set to 11640.3, Cp is 2.5, Pr is 1.0, and dynamic viscosity mu is 0. This corresponds to a gas constant R of 0.7143 and a specific heat ratio of 1.4. Use rhoCentralFoam solver.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
